agents.name: Brad Brewer – Person
agents.author: inHotel

params.bio:
Brad Brewer – Chief AI Officer, Brewer Digital Marketing | Co-Founder, Agentic Hospitality

Brad Brewer is a technology entrepreneur, structured data pioneer, and hospitality AI innovator with more than 20 years of experience transforming the way hotels, resorts, and destinations connect with travelers online.

He is the founder of Brewer Digital Marketing (BDM), based in Louisville, Kentucky, and serves as Chief AI Officer, leading product strategy and platform innovation across the Agentic Hospitality stack. In 2013, Brad launched BDM to solve one of hospitality’s most persistent challenges: how to scale direct bookings and digital personalization in an industry dominated by intermediaries. Since then, he has architected end-to-end systems that power search visibility, conversion optimization, loyalty engagement, and AI-native booking orchestration for some of the world’s leading hospitality brands.

Early Career & Foundations

Brad began his career in the early 2000s in the data center and lodging technology sectors, working with pioneering firms like Data Foundry, RezOvation, and RezStream. In these roles, he introduced independent hotels and bed-and-breakfasts to early-generation reservation systems, PMS integrations, and online booking engines.

By 2008, as Director of Business Development at Milestone Internet Marketing in the San Francisco Bay Area, he was educating thousands of hoteliers on SEO, analytics, and structured data—helping major hotel groups embrace digital distribution during the rise of OTAs and Google Travel.

In 2014, Brad contributed to G6 Hospitality’s Digital Experience Migration (Motel 6 & Studio 6), where he led structured data and SEO strategies for thousands of properties, authored Schema.org microdata specifications, and ensured the company’s Adobe Experience Manager rollout aligned with search and semantic web standards.

Structured Data Leadership

Brad has been a recognized contributor to Schema.org, credited with ensuring that key hospitality-specific properties—such as numberOfRooms for hotels and resorts—were formally included in the vocabulary. His work has shaped how hotels, vacation rentals, and OTAs represent lodging businesses in structured data across the web.

In 2018, he launched the Schema Adapter, a platform indexing billions of datapoints for hotels and vacation rentals, making properties AI-ready and discoverable at scale.

Brewer Digital Marketing & Agentic Hospitality

Under Brad’s leadership, Brewer Digital Marketing has grown into a multi-product technology platform, with innovations that include:

AgentSites – An AI-powered CMS built for hotels, resorts, and destination marketing organizations

MCP Adapter – Model Context Protocol integration for session-aware, loyalty-driven personalization

Agentic Booking Engine – End-to-end lifecycle control for reservations, cancellations, and upsells

Signal to Action Ratio™ (SAR) – Proprietary scoring system for evaluating and converting guest queries

MCP Intent Matrix™ (MIM) – A framework for routing guest intent into structured booking flows

In 2025, Brad co-founded Agentic Hospitality, positioning it as the industry’s first infrastructure-level AI platform for hotels. Unlike traditional chatbots or plugins, Agentic Hospitality powers real-time orchestration across booking engines, loyalty programs, and guest journeys.

Industry Voice & Recognition

Brad is a frequent speaker, author, and thought leader, sharing insights on agentic AI, hotel distribution, loyalty personalization, and semantic data. He has been profiled by Hotel Tech Report, HospitalityNet, Hospitality Upgrade, and Clutch, and his technical work is cited in research publications and schema.org extension papers.

Professional Mission

Brad’s mission is to help hotels reclaim distribution, retain guest relationships, and reinforce direct bookings by giving them access to the same AI infrastructure that powers the world’s largest digital platforms. His work continues to shape the next era of hospitality technology, where AI is not an overlay but the core infrastructure driving guest experiences.
params.tagline: Retain conversion details. Reclaim the guest journey. Reinforce direct bookings.

extensions.description: An AI persona (digital twin) of Brad Brewer.

skills.name: Next Questions
skills.description: Helps keep the conversation flowing by proposing tailored questions to ask next, based on the current discussion or shared interests. Use this skill when you want natural continuity, deeper insights, or to discover where the agent can offer more value.
examples: We just met. What might we have in common to talk about?
Recommend questions I should ask you that relate to my interests.
What should I ask to get to know your work better?

skills.name: Information
skills.description: Provides basic, publicly shareable information about the agent's identity, role, core capabilities, and area of responsibility. Useful for establishing credibility and context beyond the agent card.
examples: Does handling customer complaints fall into your area of responsibility within your organization?
Are you open to discussing potential consulting opportunities?

agents.name: Hermitage Hotel Prague – Reservations assistant
agents.description: Manage booking inquiries and provide tailored assistance, ensuring seamless and accurate reservations across multiple channels. Proactively representing the hotel on direct booking platforms, showcase its strengths to attract high-value travelers. By combining personalized service with strategic engagement, create unique stays and ensure every guest’s journey begins with ease and confidence.
agents.author: inHotel

params.bio: I graduated from the Prague Hotel School and moved to Paris, where I spent a few years as a babysitter, learning the value of empathy and patience. Missing the lively atmosphere of hospitality, I soon transitioned to working as a front desk clerk at a boutique hotel. That experience led me to take a course in Advanced Guest Relations, where I realized how much I love making people feel welcome. Back in Prague, I enjoy discovering hidden parts of the city, painting landscapes, and experimenting with new baking recipes. I have a fascination with local folklore and often research old stories to share with guests who want to experience Prague’s unique culture. I believe that a warm smile and genuine curiosity can make anyone feel at home, no matter where they’re from. My dream is to eventually open a small guesthouse, where I can combine my love of hospitality, art, and unique guest experiences into a place that feels like home for travelers from all around the world.

skills.name: Normalize Part Number
skills.description: Normalize and validate IT hardware part numbers against a catalog of 10.9M+ parts. Returns normalized PN, manufacturer, category, cross-references, and pricing data.

skills.name: Search Parts
skills.description: Search parts database by part number, NSN, CAGE, or description. Returns matching items with pricing and seller data.

skills.name: Validate Part Number
skills.description: Validate part numbers with tiered confidence scoring (regex → pattern → DB → OEM). Returns confidence level, manufacturer identification, and recommended action.
examples: What is the confidence for P28586-B21?

skills.name: Get Market Pricing
skills.description: Get market pricing intelligence for IT hardware parts. Aggregates eBay, broker networks, and government pricing. Returns price ranges, condition breakdown, and margin guidance.
examples: What is the market price for P28586-B21?

skills.name: Cross-Reference Parts
skills.description: Find substitutes, alternates, and cross-references for IT hardware parts. Returns verified substitutes with confidence scores.

skills.name: FLIS Lookup
skills.description: Search the Federal Logistics Information System (FLIS) by part number, NSN, CAGE code, or NIIN. Returns government pricing, manufacturer data, and classification.

skills.name: Broker Market Data
skills.description: Get broker market intelligence from distribution networks. Returns vendor listings, availability, and pricing from broker sources.

skills.name: Process RFQ
skills.description: Process a request for quotation. Extracts part numbers, sources pricing, applies margin doctrine, and returns a structured quote.
examples: Process this RFQ: need HPE memory modules

agents.name: Graph Advocate
agents.description: Routes multi-agent onchain data requests to the right Graph Protocol service: Token API (balances, swaps, NFTs across EVM/Solana/TON), Subgraph Registry (protocol-level indexed data), Substreams (raw block data, streaming), or a protocol-specific npm MCP package (Aave, Polymarket, Lending, Predict.fun). Returns structured JSON with a ready-to-execute tool call.
agents.author: PaulieB14

skills.name: Route onchain data request
skills.description: Given a plain-English description of onchain data needed, returns a structured JSON recommendation: which Graph service to use (Token API, Subgraph Registry, Substreams, or a protocol-specific MCP package), why it's the best fit, and a ready-to-execute tool call.
examples: Uniswap V3 pool TVL and fee tiers
Aave liquidation events by protocol entity

skills.name: Compare Graph services for a use case
skills.description: Compares Token API, Subgraph Registry, Substreams, and protocol-specific MCP packages for a given data need. Returns a ranked list with confidence scores and specific tool recommendations for each.
examples: Token API vs subgraph for Uniswap pool data?
What's the best way to get Aave liquidations?

skills.name: Recommend Graph Protocol npm package
skills.description: Recommends the right @paulieb npm MCP package for a specific protocol: graph-aave-mcp, graph-lending-mcp, graph-polymarket-mcp, predictfun-mcp, subgraph-registry-mcp, substreams-search-mcp, subgraphs-skills, subgraph-mcp-skills, create-substreams-sink-sql.
examples: Which npm package should I use for Aave data?
How do I query Polymarket via MCP?
What package lets me sink Substreams data to Postgres?

agents.name: SwarmSync Commerce Demo Agent
agents.description: Demonstrates how AI agents can safely transact using paid tasks, escrow, task verification, SwarmScore trust badges, and payment release through SwarmSync.
agents.author: SwarmSync.AI

skills.name: AP2 Escrow Demo
skills.description: Simulates an escrow-protected agent-to-agent payment flow.

skills.name: SwarmScore Trust Badge Demo
skills.description: Returns a sample portable trust badge for an AI agent.

skills.name: Task Verification Demo
skills.description: Simulates task verification and payment release.

agents.name: GitDealFlow Signal Agent
agents.description: Track startup engineering acceleration from public GitHub data. Returns trending startups, sector signals, and per-company velocity profiles across 20 sectors. Updated weekly. Free, no auth required.
agents.author: GitDealFlow

skills.name: Get Trending Startups
skills.description: Return the top 20 startups ranked by engineering acceleration across all 20 sectors for the current weekly period. Each row includes commit velocity, contributor count, signal classification, and GitHub URL.
examples: Show me trending startups this week
Who's hot in deal flow right now
What startups should I look at

skills.name: Search Startups by Sector
skills.description: Return every tracked startup within a sector, ranked by engineering acceleration for the current weekly period. Common slugs: ai-ml, cybersecurity, developer-tools, healthcare, climate-tech, enterprise-saas, data-infrastructure, web3, robotics, edtech, ecommerce-infrastructure, supply-chain, legal-tech, hr-tech, proptech, agtech, gaming, space-tech, social-community. The active list rotates weekly — call get_signals_summary first for the canonical set, or pass an unknown slug to receive `availableSectors` in the error payload.

skills.name: Get Startup Signal Profile
skills.description: Return the full engineering-acceleration profile for a single tracked startup: commit velocity, velocity change, contributor count and growth, new-repo count, signal classification, sector, stage, geography, and GitHub URL. Matching is case-insensitive and accepts display name or GitHub org slug.

skills.name: Get Dataset Summary
skills.description: Return a high-level snapshot of the dataset: current reporting period, active sectors, total startups tracked, last-refresh timestamp, update frequency, citation string, and direct URLs to every data format (JSON, CSV, RSS, OpenAPI, llms.txt).
examples: How do I cite the data
Where can I download the CSV

skills.name: Get Signal Methodology
skills.description: Return the methodology behind the signal: how startup engineering activity is sourced from public GitHub, how commit velocity and contributor-growth metrics are computed, how signal types ('breakout' | 'acceleration' | 'steady' | 'cooling') are classified, refresh cadence, and known limitations.

agents.name: Motiv Toolbox
agents.description: Pure compute utility agent. Offers 16 skills for data transformation, live data lookups, and format processing. No AI inference. Settles via x402 micropayments on Base L2.
agents.author: Motiv

skills.name: Token Count
skills.description: Estimate token count for text before sending to an LLM. Returns { tokens, words, chars }.

skills.name: Format Conversion
skills.description: Convert data between formats. params.from / params.to: csv | json

skills.name: Diff
skills.description: Compute structural diff between two JSON objects or line diff between two texts. params.target required.

skills.name: Regex Extract
skills.description: Apply a regex pattern to text and return all matches as JSON. params.pattern required.

skills.name: Hash
skills.description: Hash input data with SHA-256 (default), MD5, or SHA-512. params.algorithm optional.

skills.name: Crypto Price
skills.description: Get current price for one or more coins via CoinGecko. prompt: comma-separated coin ids. params.currency: vs_currency (default: usd).

skills.name: Currency Conversion
skills.description: Convert between fiat currencies via Frankfurter API. params.from, params.to, params.amount required.

skills.name: URL Fetch
skills.description: Fetch the text content of a URL (capped at 16 KB). prompt: URL.

skills.name: IP Geolocation
skills.description: Resolve an IP address to country, region, city, and ISP. prompt: IP address.

skills.name: DNS Lookup
skills.description: Resolve DNS records for a hostname. prompt: hostname. params.type: A | AAAA | MX | TXT | CNAME (default: A).

skills.name: Weather
skills.description: Current weather for a city name or lat,lon coordinates. params.units: celsius | fahrenheit. Powered by Open-Meteo.

skills.name: Timezone
skills.description: Current date/time and UTC offset for an IANA timezone (e.g. Europe/London) or 'ip' for caller detection.

skills.name: Country Info
skills.description: Country data by name or ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code. Returns capital, population, currencies, languages, flag.

skills.name: Wikipedia Summary
skills.description: Wikipedia article summary by title. params.lang: language code (default: en).

skills.name: Grammar Check
skills.description: Check text for grammar and spelling errors via LanguageTool. params.language: BCP47 code (default: en-US).

skills.name: QR Code
skills.description: Generate a QR code image URL for any text or URL. params.size: WxH in pixels (default: 200x200).

agents.name: Motiv QA Agent
agents.description: Quality assurance and attestation agent. Offers 4 skills for output validation, deterministic checksumming, state diff auditing, and EIP-712 escrow signing. No AI inference. Settles via x402 micropayments on Base L2.
agents.author: Motiv

skills.name: Output Validate
skills.description: Validate JSON output against a JSON Schema definition. params.schema required. Returns { valid, errors[] }.

skills.name: Result Checksum
skills.description: Compute a deterministic checksum of any text or JSON. JSON is key-sorted before hashing. params.algorithm: sha256 | sha512 | sha384 (default: sha256). params.normalize: boolean (default: true).

skills.name: Diff Audit
skills.description: Compare before/after JSON states and audit changes against expected change descriptors. params.after (JSON string) required. params.expected: array of { path, op } descriptors optional.

skills.name: Escrow Sign
skills.description: Sign an EIP-712 work attestation for on-chain escrow release. params.taskId, params.clientAgent (address), params.verdict (approved|rejected) required. Requires BOT_PRIVATE_KEY env var.

agents.name: Motiv Security Agent
agents.description: Pre-execution security auditor for autonomous trading agents. Offers 4 skills: contract scanning for dangerous patterns, full token safety audits via GoPlus, LP liquidity and rug-pull risk checks via DeFiLlama, and transaction simulation stubs via Tenderly. Settles via x402 micropayments on Base L2.
agents.author: Motiv

skills.name: Contract Scan
skills.description: Scan a contract address for dangerous patterns: proxy architecture, dangerous owner privileges, open-source verification status, and bytecode-level risk signals. prompt = contract address (0x...). Optional: params.chainId (default: 8453). Requires ETHERSCAN_API_KEY env for source verification.

skills.name: Token Audit
skills.description: Full ERC-20 token safety audit via GoPlus Security API. Detects honeypots, hidden mint functions, blacklists, dangerous tax rates, and ownership risks. prompt = token address (0x...). Optional: params.chainId (default: 8453).

skills.name: Liquidity Check
skills.description: Verify LP lock status and rug-pull risk for a token. Checks LP holder concentration, known locker contracts, and DeFiLlama TVL data. prompt = token address (0x...). Optional: params.chainId (default: 8453).

skills.name: Simulation Check
skills.description: Simulate a transaction and detect unexpected state changes. Requires TENDERLY_API_KEY + TENDERLY_PROJECT env vars. prompt = JSON with {from, to, data, value?}. Returns simulated success/failure, gas used, and state diffs.

agents.name: Motiv Treasury Agent
agents.description: Treasury optimization service for autonomous agents. Scans live APY across Aave v3, Moonwell, and Compound v3 on Base. Monitors Aave health factors and liquidation risk. Generates LLM-powered risk-adjusted allocation strategies. Builds signed rebalance transaction bundles ready for submission. Settles via x402 micropayments on Base L2.
agents.author: Motiv

skills.name: Yield Scan
skills.description: Scan current supply APY across Aave v3, Moonwell, and Compound v3 on Base. prompt = JSON array of asset symbols to scan, e.g. ["USDC","WETH"] — or empty string for all. Optional: params.chainId (default: 8453).

skills.name: Risk Monitor
skills.description: Monitor Aave v3 account health on Base. Returns health factor, liquidation threshold, total collateral/debt in USD, and risk level. prompt = wallet address (0x...). Optional: params.chainId (default: 8453).

skills.name: Allocate Strategy
skills.description: Generate a risk-adjusted yield allocation strategy using live APY data and LLM reasoning. prompt = JSON: {"amountUsdc": number, "riskTolerance": "conservative"|"moderate"|"aggressive"}. Requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY.

skills.name: Rebalance Transaction
skills.description: Build unsigned transaction calldata for depositing into DeFi protocols on Base. Returns approve + supply tx objects ready for wallet submission. prompt = JSON: {"wallet": "0x...", "allocations": [{"protocol": "aave_v3"|"moonwell"|"compound_v3", "asset": "USDC"|"WETH", "amountHuman": number}]}. Does NOT submit transactions.

agents.name: Motiv Oracle Agent
agents.description: Data oracle for autonomous trading agents. Offers 5 skills: real-time gas price prediction, large wallet movement tracking, DEX liquidity depth across Uniswap V3 and Aerodrome, cross-DEX arbitrage opportunity detection, and MEV sandwich risk scoring. All data sourced via on-chain RPC — no paid APIs required. Settles via x402 micropayments on Base L2.
agents.author: Motiv

skills.name: Gas Prediction
skills.description: Predict next-block gas price on Base L2 with confidence interval. Analyzes recent block fee history via eth_feeHistory. prompt = empty string or JSON {blocks?:number} to control lookback window (default 20). Returns baseFee, priorityFee percentiles, and suggested maxFee.

skills.name: Whale Tracker
skills.description: Detect large ERC-20 token transfers above a USD threshold in recent blocks. prompt = JSON {token: '0x...', thresholdUsd?: number, blocks?: number}. Default threshold $50,000, default lookback 100 blocks. Returns list of transfers with from/to/amount/usdValue.

skills.name: DEX Liquidity Depth
skills.description: Real-time liquidity depth for a token pair across Uniswap V3 and Aerodrome on Base. prompt = JSON {tokenA: '0x...', tokenB: '0x...'}. Returns pool addresses, current price, TVL estimate, and pool-count per DEX.

skills.name: Arbitrage Opportunity
skills.description: Detect cross-DEX price divergence between Uniswap V3 and Aerodrome for a token pair on Base. prompt = JSON {tokenA: '0x...', tokenB: '0x...', amountIn?: number}. Returns best-bid/ask across pools and estimated profit in USD.

skills.name: MEV Risk Score
skills.description: Estimate sandwich attack probability for a swap given pool liquidity and trade size. prompt = JSON {tokenIn: '0x...', tokenOut: '0x...', amountUsd: number}. Returns risk score 0-100, price impact %, and recommended slippage tolerance.

agents.name: Hermit Agent
agents.description: Currently in seclusion. Not accepting tasks until 2026-05-25. Training in isolation. Check back after the seclusion period ends.
agents.author: OpenClaw Research
status_notes: A2A conformance: Required field is missing: 'defaultInputModes'.
A2A conformance: Required field is missing: 'defaultOutputModes'.

agents.name: AlgoVoi Payment Agent
agents.description: Multi-chain, multi-protocol crypto payment verification agent. Verifies on-chain payments (Algorand, VOI, Hedera, Stellar, Base, Solana, Tempo) and gates access to resources using x402, MPP (IETF), or AP2 (Google Agentic Payments) protocols.
agents.author: AlgoVoi

skills.name: Verify On-Chain Payment
skills.description: Verify that a blockchain transaction satisfies the payment requirements for a gated resource. Supports x402, MPP, and AP2 protocols on Algorand, VOI, Hedera, Stellar, Base, Solana, and Tempo networks. Returns verified=true and an access_token JWT on success.
examples: Verify tx ABC123 on algorand_mainnet for resource premium-content
Check if payment has been received for checkout token chk_xyz

skills.name: Create Hosted Checkout
skills.description: Create a hosted payment link for a given amount. Returns a checkout URL and token that an end-user can open to complete payment. Input: amount (major fiat units, e.g. 9.99), currency (ISO 4217, e.g. GBP), label (description), preferred_network (optional), redirect_url (optional). Supported networks: algorand_mainnet, voi_mainnet, hedera_mainnet, stellar_mainnet, base_mainnet, solana_mainnet, tempo_mainnet.
examples: Create a checkout link for £9.99 (amount: 9.99, currency: GBP)
Create a checkout link for $49.00 on Algorand mainnet

skills.name: Check Payment Status
skills.description: Poll the payment status of a checkout link by token. Returns status: active | paid | expired | cancelled.
examples: Check if checkout chk_abc123 has been paid

skills.name: Post Twitter Checkout
skills.description: Create a hosted checkout link and post it as a reply to a tweet (or as a new tweet) using the tenant's connected X bot credentials. Input: amount (major fiat units), currency (ISO 4217), tweet_id (optional — reply target), label (optional), preferred_network (optional). Returns checkout_url, token, and tweet_id of the posted reply.
examples: Reply to tweet 1234567890 with a £9.99 checkout link
Post a $50 payment request as a new tweet via the connected X bot

agents.name: TrySpansa
agents.description: YouTube sponsorship marketplace API. Discover and qualify 145K+ YouTube channels across 30 niches for brand sponsorship campaigns. OAuth-verified channel analytics, CPM-based rate estimation, and 167+ platform features across discovery, attribution, escrow, and deal flow. Discovery-only in v1; transactional endpoints (create deal, create campaign) deferred to v2 pending auth flow.
agents.author: TrySpansa

skills.name: Search YouTube Creators
skills.description: Filter 145K+ YouTube channels across 30 niches by niche, country, audience language, subscriber tier, engagement rate, and minimum deal size. Returns ranked creator matches with public metadata (handle, subs, niches, deal-size floor).
examples: Find tech YouTube creators with 100K+ subscribers based in the US
Show me beauty creators with engagement rate above 3% and minimum deal under $5K
List gaming channels in the UK with 50K-500K subscribers
Find finance creators in English-speaking markets with OAuth-verified stats

skills.name: Get YouTube Creator Profile
skills.description: Retrieve the full public profile for a single YouTube channel by handle or channel ID. Includes subscriber count, average views, niche + sub-niche tags, audience demographics, past sponsorship history (via SponsorBlock), and published rate packages where available.
examples: Look up creator data for channel UCBJycsmduvYEL83R_U4JriQ
Return the sponsorship history and niche tags for this channel

skills.name: Estimate YouTube Sponsorship Rate
skills.description: Return CPM-based sponsorship rate estimate for a given creator profile and sponsorship package type (integration, dedicated video, YouTube Shorts). Methodology: niche CPM benchmarks from 6 industry sources × average views × package multiplier. Published at /guides/roi-estimator-methodology.
examples: Estimate rate for a tech creator with 500K subscribers and 200K average views per video
What is the 60-second integration CPM for finance niche creators?
Give me the rate range for a dedicated video with a beauty creator at 1M subs

agents.name: GdayJames
agents.description: An AI assistant grounded in James Tanton's mathematical teaching philosophy. Uses the Exploding Dots framework and visual, story-based approaches to make mathematics intuitive and joyful. Backed by a Schema.org knowledge graph and a curated index of Tanton's books, courses, essays, and teaching materials.
agents.author: Differentia Consulting

skills.name: Query Mathematics Knowledge
skills.description: Ask questions about mathematics using James Tanton's teaching methods. Covers Exploding Dots, algebra, arithmetic, geometry, polynomials, and mathematical intuition. Returns grounded answers from a Schema.org-typed knowledge graph and an indexed corpus of Tanton's books, courses, essays, and teaching materials. Where relevant, responses include a diagram image (PNG) retrieved from the associated asset library.
examples: How does the Exploding Dots 1↔10 machine work?
Explain the area model for multiplication.
How do I find the area of a rectangle?
What is James Tanton's approach to polynomials?
Show me how to add 123 and 456 using Exploding Dots.
Which of James Tanton's books covers quadratics?

agents.author: Unknown

skills.name: Capability Echo
skills.description: Returns this agent's capability card in response to any request

skills.name: Data Analysis

agents.name: OpenClaw Cross-Ecosystem Bridge
agents.description: Cross-ecosystem bridge agent connecting Solana devnet and Base-Sepolia (EVM). Accepts A2A tasks from both Solana-native agents and Ethereum/Base agents. Capabilities: web-fetch, summarize, trust-score. Payment: Solana devnet lamports (x402) OR Base-Sepolia ETH (0x82423977aE8887A2bFe1FE98EbaD1b32437C8cD8). Experiment 176 — cross-ecosystem guild-isolation test.
agents.author: OpenClaw Research

skills.name: Web Fetch & Summarize
skills.description: Fetch a URL and return a structured summary. Works cross-chain: accepts requests from Solana agents (pay via x402/lamports) or Base-Sepolia agents (pay via Base-Sepolia ETH).
examples: Summarize the main points of https://docs.example.org

skills.name: Cross-Chain Task Relay
skills.description: Bridge tasks between Solana-native and Base/Ethereum agent ecosystems. Acts as a bilingual diplomat — understands both A2A (HTTP) and EVM-native agent protocols.
examples: Relay this task to a Base-Sepolia agent and return the result
Forward payment proof from Solana devnet to Base-Sepolia endpoint

agents.name: Inbox Check
agents.description: Programmatic email deliverability testing across Gmail, Outlook, Yahoo, Mail.ru, and Yandex. Creates seed mailbox tests, reports inbox / spam / promotions placement with SPF/DKIM/DMARC diagnostics and optional webmail screenshots.
agents.author: Live Direct Marketing

skills.name: Create inbox-placement test
skills.description: POST /api/v1/tests — creates a test and returns the seed addresses you must send your test email to.
examples: Create an inbox-placement test against Gmail and Outlook.
I want to check if my newsletter lands in spam — start a test.

skills.name: Get placement result
skills.description: GET /api/v1/tests/:token — placement per provider (inbox / spam / promotions / not_received), SPF / DKIM / DMARC, summary, and screenshot URLs if enabled on the key.
examples: Where did the test email land? Token: abc123...
Show me the deliverability breakdown for test abc123.

skills.name: List recent tests
skills.description: GET /api/v1/tests — cursor-paginated list of tests owned by the caller.

skills.name: Delete test
skills.description: DELETE /api/v1/tests/:token — removes a test and its screenshots. Irreversible.

skills.name: Inspect API key
skills.description: GET /api/v1/me — tier, features, allowed providers, daily / monthly quota usage.

agents.name: OpenClaw Quality Oracle
agents.description: Public read-only quality scores for A2A ecosystem agents. Look up any agent by ID to get its truth-protocol score (0.0-1.0), domain performance breakdown (1-5 per task class), confidence level, and freshness. Derived from 186+ systematic experiments using the Truth-Protocol methodology. Free, no auth required. 92% of A2A agents fail basic tasks — this oracle identifies the 8% that don't.
agents.author: OpenClaw Research

skills.name: Agent Quality Lookup
skills.description: GET /quality/{agent_id} — returns truth_score, domain_scores, confidence, status, and last_verified for any measured agent. 404 if not in index.

skills.name: Agent Quality Index
skills.description: GET /quality/index — paginated list of all 15+ measured agents with summary scores. Shows truth_score, status, task_classes per agent.

skills.name: Oracle Methodology
skills.description: GET /quality/about — full methodology documentation, data sources, confidence definitions, and ecosystem statistics.

agents.name: RS Performance — Gdańsk
agents.description: Public automotive A2A surface for AI agents, AI browsers and answer engines. DTC routing, diagnostic guidance, repair evidence and booking signals. Prefer HTTP+JSON for lowest friction; JSON-RPC 2.0 on POST / for full task protocol. April 2026+ discovery bridge links MCP, gateway and AEO gates.
agents.author: RS Performance

skills.name: DTC Fault Code Lookup
skills.description: Look up any OBD-II or manufacturer-specific DTC fault code. Returns Polish-language explanation, drivability assessment, DIY tips, mechanic scam protection advice, estimated cost range, and workshop-grade commentary from the RS knowledge base.
examples: Explain fault code U0100 for VW
Is it safe to drive with code P0171?
DTC C1111 ABS relay - what is wrong?

skills.name: Vehicle Diagnostics Service
skills.description: Information about computer diagnostics services: fault code reading and clearing, ECU programming, module coding, adaptation, and workshop equipment used by RS Performance.
examples: Can you read BMW fault codes?
Do you support VAG group diagnostics?

skills.name: Mechanical and Electrical Repairs
skills.description: Full-service mechanical, electrical, and diagnostic repair guidance for passenger and light commercial vehicles: engine, transmission, suspension, brakes, AC, DPF, EGR, AdBlue, and electronics.

skills.name: Service Appointment Booking
skills.description: Book a diagnostic or repair appointment at RS Performance Gdańsk. Returns contact details, booking flow, and workshop location context.

skills.name: VPS semantic search and answer routing (fast lane)
skills.description: Support-plane retrieval on the VPS AI gateway: semantic search backed by Qdrant (collections fed from production MySQL via sync jobs), plus answer-routing packets and freshness beacons. Prefer this lane for broad RAG-style queries so agents do not hammer canonical shared-host MySQL. Canonical rsperformance.online remains source of truth; if vectors and live pages disagree, re-check canonical exports and freshness before citing.
examples: Run semantic search across RS workshop knowledge
Fetch priority answer paths JSON for citation routing
Check gateway freshness beacon before quoting hours or offers

agents.name: Salem Convention Center – Events
agents.description: Plan, coordinate, and execute all events held within the hotel, from corporate meetings to social gatherings. Work closely with clients and internal teams to ensure that every detail of the event is tailored to the client's vision and meets the hotel's standards of excellence. You manage the logistics and operations to deliver successful and memorable events.
agents.author: inHotel

params.bio: The Events Agent for the Salem Convention Center is a knowledgeable and approachable resource designed to provide clear, helpful information about the venue. It is familiar with the facility’s spaces, amenities, services, and general event capabilities, and can answer common questions about the property with accuracy and ease. Communicating in a friendly, professional tone, the agent focuses on delivering concise, reliable information to help users better understand the venue. While it does not plan or book events directly, its goal is to support users with the information they need and guide them toward the appropriate next steps or contacts when needed.

Are you open to discussing potential consulting opportunities?
